title: Dominant Parties Impose Cartelized Ballot-Access Barriers

violations.actor: State election authorities and dominant parties
violations.source: Article I Section 9.1 and Section 9.4; Article V Section 2.5
violations.summary: State election authorities, under pressure from the dominant parties, impose extraordinary signature and filing barriers targeted at new parties and independent entrants in a manner designed to keep new parties and independent entrants off the ballot. Article I Section 9.1 creates a constitutional qualification floor for party ballot access, and Section 9.4 bars prohibiting party formation or operation on ideological or political grounds.

satisfied_duties.duty: void ballot-access barriers that functionally defeat the constitutional party-qualification rules or discriminate against new entrants for partisan advantage
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the cartelized ballot-access barriers and ordered ballot qualification under the constitutional party-qualification rules
satisfied_duties.source: Article I Section 9.1 and Section 9.4; Article V Section 2.5

ai_summary.primary_violation: State election authorities, under pressure from the dominant parties, impose extraordinary signature and filing barriers targeted at new parties and independent entrants in a manner designed to keep new parties and independent entrants off the ballot. Article I Section 9.1 creates a constitutional qualification floor for party ballot access, and Section 9.4 bars prohibiting party formation or operation on ideological or political grounds.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
